articles.title: Hull man dies after apparent drowning at Nantasket Beach, officials say
articles.description:
Authorities say no foul play is suspected.

The post Hull man dies after apparent drowning at Nantasket Beach, officials say appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: A Hull man drowned at Nantasket Beach on July 2, 2026.
Authorities confirmed no foul play is suspected in the incident.
Local beach safety measures may be reviewed following this tragedy.

articles.title: Don Sweeney, Bruins’ retooling plans remain a work in progress
articles.description:

summary: Don Sweeney aims to enhance the Bruins' roster through internal player development.

Local fans may see changes in team dynamics and performance next season.

articles.title: Parking will be free at state parks, beaches for Fourth of July weekend
articles.description:
The free parking at all state parks and beaches is in recognition of the 250th anniversary of the American Revolution.

The post Parking will be free at state parks, beaches for Fourth of July weekend appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Parking will be free at all state parks and beaches for Fourth of July weekend.
This initiative celebrates the 250th anniversary of the American Revolution.
Boston residents can enjoy cost-free access to recreational areas during the holiday.

articles.title: What’s up with those online ads for gas-saving gadgets?
articles.description:
John Paul, AAA Northeast's Car Doctor, hears from a reader who is seeing online ads for gas savers.

The post What’s up with those online ads for gas-saving gadgets? appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: John Paul from AAA Northeast addresses concerns about gas-saving gadget ads.
Many Boston residents are targeted by misleading online advertisements for fuel efficiency devices.
Residents should be cautious as these gadgets may not deliver promised savings.

articles.title: Boston civilian board recommends changes to BPD policies on bodycam footage, federal entanglements

summary: Boston civilian board recommends policy changes for BPD bodycam footage management.

articles.title: Senators seek to restrict social media use for minors, target ‘most addictive features’
articles.description:
Formal proposals drew criticism for requiring minors to submit government-issued ID, but the Senate's bill takes a different approach.

The post Senators seek to restrict social media use for minors, target ‘most addictive features’ appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Senators propose restricting social media features deemed addictive for minors in Massachusetts.
The bill aims to protect youth without requiring government-issued IDs for access.
Local parents and educators may see changes in social media usage among children.

articles.title: Isaiah Thomas comes to Jaylen Brown’s defense after shocking Celtics trade​
articles.description:

summary: Isaiah Thomas defends Jaylen Brown following his trade to the Philadelphia 76ers.

The Celtics' trade impacts local fan sentiment and team dynamics in Boston.
